text:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
62-year-old female with newly diagnosed left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma (EGFR L858R-mutant, Stage IIIB, T3N3M0) presenting for staging evaluation. Recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis demonstrated a 4.2 cm spiculated left upper lobe mass with ipsilateral mediastinal and left supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. PET-CT confirmed FDG-avid primary mass and regional nodal disease without evidence of distant metastatic disease. MRI brain obtained as part of comprehensive staging workup to evaluate for occult intracranial metastases prior to initiation of definitive systemic therapy. No neurological symptoms reported.

TECHNIQUE:
Multiparametric MRI of the brain was performed on a 3.0 Tesla scanner. Standard sequences obtained including sagittal T1 MPRAGE, axial T2 FLAIR, axial T2 FSE, axial DWI with ADC mapping, axial SWI (susceptibility-weighted imaging), and axial GRE T2* sequences. Pre- and post-contrast T1-weighted images (inversion recovery-prepared volumetric acquisition) were obtained following intravenous administration of gadobutrol (Gadavist) 0.1 mmol/kg. Multiplanar reformatted images reviewed. No prior MRI brain available for comparison.

FINDINGS:

Supratentorial Brain Parenchyma:
The cerebral cortex and underlying white matter demonstrate normal signal characteristics on all sequences. There is no abnormal T2 or FLAIR signal hyperintensity within the cerebral hemispheres to suggest vasogenic edema, leptomeningeal infiltration, or parenchymal infiltrative process. The gray-white matter differentiation is preserved throughout. No areas of restricted diffusion are identified to suggest acute ischemic change. No susceptibility artifact to suggest hemorrhage, hemosiderin deposition, or calcification beyond expected physiologic distribution in the basal ganglia.

Post-contrast sequences demonstrate no focal enhancing parenchymal lesions within the cerebral hemispheres. Specifically, there are no ring-enhancing or solid-enhancing nodules identified in the frontal, temporal, parietal, or occipital lobes bilaterally. The corticomedullary junction is carefully evaluated and appears unremarkable without nodular enhancement.

Infratentorial Structures / Posterior Fossa:
The cerebellum is of normal signal and morphology bilaterally. The cerebellar hemispheres, vermis, and dentate nuclei appear unremarkable. No focal lesions identified pre- or post-contrast. The brainstem, including the midbrain, pons, and medulla, is normal in signal and caliber. No enhancing lesions are detected within the posterior fossa.

Ventricular System and CSF Spaces:
The lateral ventricles are symmetric and of normal morphology. The third and fourth ventricles are normal in size and position. There is no evidence of hydrocephalus or obstructive pathology. The basilar cisterns are patent and unremarkable. The cortical sulci and cerebral fissures are appropriate for patient age with mild symmetric global parenchymal volume loss consistent with age-related involution, without focal atrophy.

Leptomeninges:
Following gadolinium administration, there is no abnormal leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement identified. No dural-based enhancing lesions. The interhemispheric fissure, sylvian fissures, and posterior fossa meningeal surfaces show normal enhancement pattern confined to the dura only, without sulcal or cisternal enhancement to suggest leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.

Vascular Structures:
The major intracranial arterial flow voids are preserved. The carotid siphons, middle cerebral arteries, anterior cerebral arteries, basilar artery, and posterior cerebral arteries appear unremarkable by flow void assessment on standard sequences. No vascular malformation identified.

Sella / Parasellar Region:
The pituitary gland is normal in size and signal. The pituitary stalk is midline. No sellar or parasellar mass identified.

Skull Base and Calvarium:
The osseous structures of the calvarium and skull base appear intact. No lytic or blastic bony lesions identified. The visualized portions of the orbits, paranasal sinuses, and mastoid air cells appear grossly unremarkable, with mild bilateral maxillary sinus mucosal thickening noted as an incidental finding of no clinical consequence.

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease. There are no enhancing parenchymal lesions, no dural-based metastases, and no leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement to suggest carcinomatous meningitis.

2. No evidence of acute intracranial abnormality including hemorrhage, infarction, or mass effect.

3. Mild symmetric age-appropriate cerebral parenchymal volume loss, otherwise unremarkable brain parenchyma.

4. Incidental mild bilateral maxillary sinus mucosal thickening, likely chronic sinusitis, clinically insignificant.

Overall, this is a normal MRI of the brain without gadolinium and with gadolinium contrast. In the context of the patient's known stage IIIB left lung adenocarcinoma, the absence of intracranial metastatic involvement is confirmed on this dedicated high-resolution MRI examination.
